feed.title: Howl Street — Global Financial Wire
feed.subtitle: Loudest Howls and the Pack — auto-curated from 100+ global sources.

entry.title: [LOUDEST HOWL] Oil prices rise despite Iran’s proposal to reopen Strait of Hormuz #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: Brent crude rises more than 1 percent despite Iran's offer to reopen waterway in exchange for deferral of nuclear talks.

entry.title: LPG business keeps Pryce January-March profit at P1B #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Pryce Corp. posted a profit of P1.084 billion in the first quarter, driven by its strong liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) business. The January-March result was slightly higher than the P1.062 billion recorded in the same period last year. Pryce’s revenues rose by 3.39 percent to P5.98 billion from P5.79 billion. In a

entry.title: PAL profit up 2.6% in turbulent first quarter #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Philippine Airlines (PAL) weathered a turbulent first quarter to log a 2.6-percent increase in net income, which reached to $78.55 million. This as passenger traffic continued to grow despite airspace disruptions and rising fuel costs linked to the war in the Middle East. READ: PAL seeks wider Alaska Airlines tie-up In a

entry.title: Atlas Mining turns around with P645-M profit in Q1 #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Atlas Consolidated and Mining and Development Corp. reported a net income of P645 million in the first quarter of 2025. This reversed a net loss of P404 million a year ago, driven by higher metal prices. In a disclosure on Tuesday, Atlas Mining said revenues rose by 11 percent to P4.51 billion.

entry.title: Peso touches new low of 60.97:$1 in AM trade; 61.08:$1 in PM session #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: UPDATED FOR AFTERNOON TRADING MANILA, Philippines — The Philippines’s peso slid to a record low on Tuesday, breaching the 61-per-dollar level for the first time in intraday trading READ: Peso depreciates to 60.48:$1 on Middle East war jitters In the afternoon session, the local currency touched an intraday low of 61.08, marking a fresh all-time

entry.title: Apollo Global Capital expands mining business #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Holding firm Apollo Global Capital Inc. has inked a deal with Masbate 2145 Philippines Inc. to significantly expand its presence in the local mining industry. The listed firm signed the operating agreement with Masbate 2145 through its offshore mining subsidiary, JDVC Resources Corp. The agreement covers a 15,909.33-hectare mineral property situated in

entry.title: Hotel101 Madrid racks up P387M bookings in strong debut #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Hotel101-Madrid has generated over 45,000 room night bookings worth 5.44 million euros (about P387 million), signaling strong demand since opening in March. In a disclosure on Tuesday, the company founded by tycoons Tony Tan Caktiong and Edgar “Injap” Sia II said the 680-hotel room is now producing recurring revenues. This marks a

entry.title: EUR/JPY Price Forecast: Moves below 186.50, nine-day confluence #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: EUR/JPY depreciates after two days of gains, trading around 186.40 during European hours on Tuesday. The technical analysis of the daily chart indicates the currency cross slips below the ascending channel, signaling a possible bearish reversal.

entry.title: European shares edge lower as US-Iran talks stall; corporate earnings on tap #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: European shares edge lower as US-Iran talks stall; corporate earnings on tap

entry.title: ASX hits three-week low in war limbo; energy stocks gain #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: The Australian sharemarket fell for a sixth day as uncertainty lingers about a resolution of two-month Iran war and ahead of a key inflation report.

entry.title: BP profits more than double as Iran war sends oil prices higher #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: The energy giant said it had seen an "exceptional" performance at its oil trading business.

entry.title: Earnings call transcript: Holmen AB sees mixed results in Q1 2026 #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: Earnings call transcript: Holmen AB sees mixed results in Q1 2026

entry.title: Japanese Yen pares hawkish BoJ-inspired gains; USD/JPY rebounds from sub-159.00 levels #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: The USD/JPY pair attracts some intraday selling after the Bank of Japan (BoJ) announced its policy decision and touches a one-week low earlier this Tuesday.

entry.title: Earnings call transcript: Zalaris ASA sees mixed Q1 2026, stock rises 0.41% #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: Earnings call transcript: Zalaris ASA sees mixed Q1 2026, stock rises 0.41%

entry.title: IndiGo flight lands in Guangzhou as airline resumes direct India-China services #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: Move comes amid warming bilateral ties with Air China and China Eastern also restarting services

entry.title: FTSE 100: Stocks dip as Trump rejects Iran Hormuz proposal, oil uncertainty weighs #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets

entry.title: BOJ keeps rates steady but hawkish split points to June hike #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets

entry.title: Citizens initiates Opus Genetics stock coverage with outperform rating #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets

entry.title: ‘The cost of compute is far beyond the costs of the employees’: Nvidia executive says right now AI is more expensive than paying human workers #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
entry.summary: Big Tech has announced $740 billion in capex this year, but AI has yet to show evidence of widespread increased productivity.

entry.title: BOJ Governor Ueda's comments at news conference #HowlStreet #GlobalMarkets
